headline: Cape Verde first-baseline rotation assessment. Published score 62.5 (Established). Research confirms baseline pattern: same-sex relations not criminalized; sexual-orientation employment protections; UN LGBTI Core Group member; Strategic Sustainable Development Plan 2022-2026 (PEDS II) integrating SDG and human-rights indicators; National Adaptation Plan 2022-2030 aligned with NDC; HRC dialogue raised concerns about anti-discrimination legislation, gender-based violence, court system, and corruption. Climate-adaptation infrastructure positive; corruption and gender-based violence pressure modest negatives. Net assessment: composite ~63.0 vs published 62.5 — sub-threshold delta of +0.5; band Established sustained.
key_evidence: Same-sex sexual relations not criminalized; employment protections based on sexual orientation in place.
UN LGBTI Core Group member; in top 10 most inclusive middle-income countries per Homophobic Climate Index.
Strategic Sustainable Development Plan 2022-2026 (PEDS II): SDG-aligned; integrates human-rights indicators; aims to 'leave no one behind.'
National Adaptation Plan 2022-2030 fully aligned with Nationally Determined Contribution (2021).
Government plans to adopt minorities anti-discrimination law covering LGBTI and persons with disabilities.
OHCHR HRC dialogue raised concerns: anti-discrimination legislation gaps, gender-based violence prevalence, court system efficiency, corruption.
Small-island developing state with significant climate vulnerability; IFRC 2026 country plan active.

evidence.finding: HRC experts raised concerns about anti-discrimination legislation, gender-based violence, court system, and corruption.
Cape Verde delegation reiterated commitment to freedom; planned anti-discrimination law for LGBTI and disability communities.
NDC 3.0 published 2025; long-horizon climate adaptation framework with intergenerational orientation.
UPR called for safe working environment for women and LGBTI+ defenders and journalists.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.5 unchanged. SDG-aligned planning indicates structured suffering-detection at policy level; minor gap on disaggregated data.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 unchanged. Climate adaptation infrastructure documented; gender-based violence response capacity is the main gap.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.1 (+0.1). EQ1 Universality and EQ4 Access Design strong via LGBTI inclusion; minor uplift for planned anti-discrimination law extension to disability community. Anchor moves modestly.
scoring_rationale.BND: 4.0 unchanged. B3 Scope Clarity strongly evidenced via PEDS II and NAP transparency.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.5 unchanged. HRC concerns remain partially unaddressed.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.6 (+0.1). S2 Long-Term Impact: NDC 3.0 publication 2025 demonstrates 10+ year horizon planning. S5 Coalitional Compassion: UN LGBTI Core Group leadership for small-state advocacy.

negativeSignals: Gender-based violence prevalence; corruption concerns; court system efficiency gaps per HRC dialogue.
positiveSignals: LGBTI rights infrastructure; UN LGBTI Core Group membership; NDC 3.0 long-horizon climate planning; SDG-aligned national strategy; planned minorities anti-discrimination law.
sector_context: Cape Verde is one of the highest-scoring African countries in the published index; assessment confirms that posture. Among small-island developing states, Cape Verde's combination of LGBTI inclusion and climate-adaptation planning represents a positive cluster.
open_actions_resolved: NDC 3.0 long-horizon planning captured under SYS
open_actions_carried: Re-queue for next rotation cycle to monitor: planned anti-discrimination law adoption; gender-based violence response capacity; corruption-mitigation measures
